data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe, overlay) for the docs-only plan "Docs: gate behavior, config surface, fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern": CLEAN — no T8 findings.

The overlay applies only at one remove: this is a DOCUMENTATION plan ABOUT an LLM sub-system (the three-pass gate + the Pass-4 affirmative coach), not a plan that DEFINES new prompts/schemas/enums. T8's job here is to confirm that every schema/enum/protocol the docs are tasked to document resolves to a concrete, fully-specified vocabulary an author can faithfully transcribe (an under-specified referent would make AC1/AC3 unsatisfiable). I tool-grounded each referent; all resolve.

Tool-grounded confirmation (no proven structural gap on any of the five T8 probes):

(a) No schema/enum referenced whose value vocabulary is undefined.
- "the three-pass" gate + "the Pass-4 affirmative coach" → fully enumerated in docs/plan-review-gate.md:49–139 (the four-pass table at :76–81, the Pass-3 math at :83–95) and realized in code (passes.py, orchestrator.MOVE_REGISTRY).
- "the config surface (risk posture, chunking, budget caps, per-criterion blocking, the global enable/disable kill-switch)" → the config vocabulary is concrete: verify.require_plan_review_for_claim (docs/plan-review-gate.md:176–197); block_threshold / posture / per-criterion blocking (det_floor + criteria_routing). NOTE (not a T8 gap): the plan names a "global enable/disable kill-switch," but the canonical doc states one is NOT needed ("Turning it off is the rollback — an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed", :180). That is a doc-CONTENT reconciliation for the consistency/correctness pass, not an undefined-enum structural gap — the vocabulary IS defined; the plan and the doc just disagree on whether to call it a kill-switch.
- "the move registry" / "reference-by-id" → moves 1-9,11,12 fully enumerated with locked templates (docs/plan-review-gate.md:112–124; orchestrator.MOVE_REGISTRY); finding-`id` owner is named (ca03 mints, the coach consumes) per 75a9.

(b) Processing protocol/decision-rule co-located with the schema that needs it: the Pass-3 decision rules sit beside the validity/impact/priority schema (:83–101); the C1 SUBJECT VALIDATOR (REJECT⇒no-coaching) sits beside the move-registry render rule (:128–130).

(c) No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement: there are no counters in the docs surface; cap ownership is explicitly disambiguated (Pass-3 owns the top-N cap + block decision; "the cap can never weaken the block decision", :136–139).

(d) Fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step IS specified — and is itself a documented topic: fail-open behavior is fully enumerated (docs/plan-review-gate.md:199–210: unsupported stack/missing tool/parse/timeout → abstain-as-PASS; LLM unavailable → DET-only; claim gate fails closed when signing is unavailable). The coach's own best-effort drop (rejected subject / no-move ⇒ no coaching) is specified in 75a9.

(e) No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern, and referenced agents/docs exist: the cross-referenced workflow-engine usage doc is 6f2d WS-DOC (OPEN epic — the canonical docs/workflow-engine.md is its pending deliverable; the current authoritative refs workflow-authoring-v2.md + workflow-editor.md exist), and 75a9 (the Pass-4 coach) is CLOSED/specified. The "name-the-move-not-the-answer" property is baked into the human-authored deterministic template, not LLM judgment, so the docs describe a positive-by-construction mechanism with no load-bearing negative instruction.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 criteria this chunk passes):
- No schema/enum referenced whose value vocabulary is never defined — every documented referent (three/four passes, config keys, move registry, finding-id) resolves to a concrete vocabulary in docs/plan-review-gate.md + code (tool-verified).
- The decision-rule is co-located with its schema (Pass-3 math + decision rules; subject validator + move template).
- No ambiguous counter/state-increment placement; cap/block ownership is explicit.
- A fallback for failed/incomplete sub-steps is specified (fail-open is in fact one of the documented topics).
- No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern; the cross-referenced docs/agents (6f2d, 75a9) exist and the coaching property is template-baked, not LLM-judgment-dependent.

(Per the T8 ANTI-FP clause: a well-specified plan I simply can't execute is a PASS — and here the referents are not merely plan-asserted but tool-confirmed against the canonical doc and code. No structural gap is provable, so emitting no T8 finding is correct. The kill-switch naming mismatch is logged above as a non-T8 note for the consistency pass.)
